maturity_assessment: Score each CTEM stage from 1-5 using current evidence, not aspiration.
Name the highest-friction handoff between security, IT, engineering, and business owners.
Choose one metric that proves exposure reduction, not activity volume.
crown_jewel_worksheet: Name the business service and executive owner.
Identify crown-jewel assets, privileged identities, and externally reachable paths.
Define what exposure duration is unacceptable for this service.
List resolver teams and change windows before prioritization begins.
prioritization_rationale: Start from business service impact and attacker path, then add vulnerability severity.
Escalate known exploited, internet-reachable, or identity-amplifying exposures.
Downgrade isolated findings when validation shows no plausible path to scoped services.
validation_evidence_pack: State the validation method and safety boundary.
Capture exploitability, reachability, control performance, and blast radius evidence.
Attach retest criteria to every Act or Attend remediation action.

raci.responsibility: Accountable for CTEM outcomes and risk acceptance policy.
Defines service criticality, impact tolerance, and remediation tradeoffs.
Owns scoring logic, validation evidence, and exposure quality.
Owns remediation delivery and retest readiness.
Tracks exceptions, control evidence, and executive reporting.

roadmap_30_60_90.actions: Run crown-jewel workshop, publish exposure taxonomy, and agree on Act/Attend/Monitor/Track definitions.
Normalize scanner/cloud/identity data, run safe validation on top paths, and attach evidence packs to tickets.
Operationalize SLAs, risk acceptance, retest, executive dashboards, and recurring CTEM review cadence.
